post.content: I have Uber Phone screen in 6 days. Can anyone let me know what would be best way to prepare for it? If anyone has given the interview please let me know the process. Do they ask resume projects also? And does Uber asks Hard Leetcode?

The first phone screen is usually a hard question.  It would be helpful to practice hard questions from the Uber-tagged problems to get a flavor.  I would also practice some hard questions on backtracking, bfs, dfs (grid search, topological sort- course scheduling), tree serialization/deserialization, dynamic programming and union-find. It is important to cover the breadth rather than go in-depth on any topic. Also, understand the concept really well. The question may be slightly changed. For example, they may want to serialize the tree in a certain way or you may need to also enumerate all possible ways/paths for a bfs/dfs etc. 

They will begin with your intro and can then ask for some details on a project on your resume that might interest them. This will be the first 10-15 minutes only though.

To summarize, select 1-2 hard questions from each topic, understand it well, and practice another 1-2 questions for that topic. Take a good night's sleep before the interview and make sure your brain is active during the interview. If it goes into a panic/frozen state then it is ok to take a pause for a minute and focus on long deep breaths to revive. Best of luck and don't forget to update the question type you were asked.

I don\'t know the exact answer for your question. But, I have a small suggestion for you. Don\'t try to give any mock interviews. It will lower your confidence. So, go through all the basics and previously asked questions in the Uber interviews. All The Best Buddy :)
Did you have codesignal OA round as well? If yes, what was your score?
Hey, how did your phone screen round go? what was the difficulty level? questions were on what topics? I\'ve my phone screen in next 5 days
so how did it go? could you provide any details about the questions asked?
